programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Harpers Ferry in West Virginia in the first of a two-part visit in which he tries to save the Towns Inn, a historic hotel run by retired teacher and junk hoarder Karan Townsend.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon continues to restore the failing Towns Inn in Harpers Ferry, West Virginia. After witnessing abysmal food hygiene practices in the restaurant, Gordon is left in disbelief as he uncovers terrifying cleanliness issues throughout the hotel.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In West London, dance-mad newlyweds Keon and Zoe are taking their first step onto the London property ladder and are looking for a family home with space for their favourite hobby: salsa-dancing.

programme.title: Stacey Solomons Renovation Rescue
programme.desc: Show host Stacey Soloman helps a multigenerational household to create the spacious kitchen diner theyve always dreamt of, and finishes off her own Mediterranean haven poolside project.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth host in Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, tech entrepreneur Shane, is hoping to wow his guests with a menu that draws on his Jamaican heritage. His ambitious menu flirts with triumph, and his strong gin martinis also raise the bar.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jordan and Robin host the weeks final visit at their country pub, The Drayton Crown, in Somerset. But with one BandBer dreading the potential of stale ale smell, the hosts have their work cut out.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Spain, a night on the tiles proves too much for holiday hosts Daniel and John. And will winter come too soon for Angie and Ashs Italian ski chalets?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Newly-weds Bev and Errol cant wait to buy themselves the ultimate wedding present - their very own holiday home in Mar Menor, Spain. Can Jasmine Harman find them the one for their £65k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Are best friends Sarah and Jo enjoying the fruits of their labour at their Corfu olive grove house? And we see how Hannah and Gareth are getting on in Salobreña.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Harrogate, the first to host is fun farm girl, 52-year-old Jane Steele. Jane plans a meal featuring twists on classic dishes and some fancy wine to wash it down.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Cassy Bell hopes her jockey-themed evening will put her in the running to win the cash prize. Shes confident that her gastro-pub food is just the ticket - just so long as her trusty chicken timer doesnt let her down.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Veteran punk turned cupcake seller Cathy McConaghy has decided to go back to her Irish roots on day three in Harrogate, with a menu including homemade soda bread, cockle soup, oysters, roast lamb and stout-infused sticky toffee pudding.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer and all round cool cat Simon Meyer takes a laid-back approach to hosting in Harrogate. Simons chicken soup starter goes down almost too well as his guests kind comments leave an emotional Simon struggling to hold it together.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final night from in and around Harrogate finds local toff Alasdair Moore looking to impress his guests with smart wines and `proper dinner party food. However, an early slip up sees Alasdairs confidence fall away.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lian and Dave are searching for a holiday home in Spains Almería Province to enjoy with their 13-year-old son Dominic. Ben Hillman lines up five impressive properties their £80,000 budget.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George takes inspiration from Japan, to help Rachel and Sarah with their Victorian terrace. And have a young couple taken on too much with a listed 17th-century cottage?

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start. But Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny follows brave buyers transforming properties bought at auction from derelict to diamond. Alison and Michael take on a pitfall palace in Guildford, while Jules and Al tackle a creaky Cornwall seven-bed BandB.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Moors Murders
programme.desc: The story of Britains most infamous child murders, by serial killers Ian Brady and Myra Hindley. Featuring an eyewitness account of the last murder, from Hindleys brother-in-law David Smith. He alerted police and brought the murders to an end.

programme.title: Moors Murders
programme.desc: The police arrest Ian Brady for a single murder. The discovery of a horrific tape recording and photos lead to a search on the moors, revealing a string of victims, and Myra Hindley as an accomplice.

programme.title: Moors Murders
programme.desc: In 1966, Brady and Hindley are brought to court. Eyewitnesses describe the trial, as letters reveal the serial killers carceral after lives as Britains longest serving prisoners.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: In Old California
programme.desc: Tom Craig is a mild-mannered druggist from Boston who opens a shop in wild and woolly Sacramento shortly before the Gold Rush. The town is `owned by the Dawson brothers, Britt and Joe, who poison Craigs tonic when saloon hostess Lacey Miller takes too much of an interest in the handsome newcomer. Town drunk Whitey has one drink too many, and all of Sacramento is soon in a lynching mood.

programme.desc: Tha Bing a dèanamh briosgaidean ach gun duine cluinntinn an ùineadair, tha iad a losgadh. Bing ends up burning his biscuits when he fails to hear the oven timer.

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach
programme.desc: Tusk is painting a portrait of Ping Wing. Well, hes trying to. Frustrated, he throws away his attempts.

programme.title: S E Iasg a ThAnnam
programme.desc: A jellyfish explains how he moves around the sea without a skeleton, heart or brain. He also demonstrates how he uses his tentacles to catch his food and ward off predators.

programme.title: Cleasan Ataidh and Tilidh
programme.desc: Abney and Teal construct a dragon. Neep puts on a brave face, but really he isnt sure he wants to share the island with such a scary monster.

programme.title: Peicein
programme.sub-title: Oh No, I Dont Want My Brain to Get Wet!
programme.desc: Petit doesnt want to put his head underwater because hes sure that his brain will get wet. Laura tells him that she thinks hes afraid of diving and discovering the wonderful world that lies at the bottom of the pool.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Iain MacInnes reads "Measgaichte".

programme.title: An Doimhne
programme.desc: Cà l a h-uile duine bhon Orca Dhorch? Wheres everyone from the Dark Orca?

programme.title: An Sgoil
programme.desc: Its the day of the big concert at Sgoil Taobh na Cloiche.

programme.title: Dubhlain CBBC Alba
programme.desc: Ellen NicDhòmhnaill le sùil air ais air cuid de na dùbhlain a chaidh fheuchainn air CBBC ALBA bho chionn ghoirid. Ellen Macdonald takes a look at some of the crazy challenges attempted by the CBBC ALBA team, including some tricky upside-down noodle-lifting.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Dave and his friends are given a lesson in responsibility when their school assigns them an animal each to look after overnight.

programme.title: Golf: Mun Cuairt Alba
programme.desc: Gary Dingwall shows us how to play the challenging 14th at Royal Dornoch called Foxy, and professional coach Keil Beveridge demonstrates his weekly top playing tip.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Historian Dr Martin Macgregor visits the studio and Calum Maclean is at Rosal Clearance Village in Mackay Country, Sutherland.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Rathad Ramsay
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon takes a stroll on the beach with Ingrid Henderson. Tha Ramsay MacMahon agabhail cuairt air an tràigh cuide ri Ingrid NicEanraig.

programme.title: Cuantan Caochlaideach
programme.desc: In Europes North and Baltic Seas, eider duck chicks flee attacking gulls, while sea trout struggle to return to the rivers of their birth.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.sub-title: Overnight Oats le Sùbhagan is Piostaisean
programme.desc: Tha Abigail a dèanamh Overnight Oats le Sùbhagan is Piostaisean. Abigail makes raspberry and pistachio overnight oats.

programme.title: Seòid a Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads are inspired to cook with a tandoor oven. After visiting chef Anil for some expert advice in a local hotel kitchen, they head back to their barn kitchen and their new oven.

programme.title: Sar Sgeoil
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald on George Mackay Browns prophetic novel, Greenvoe.

programme.title: OMC!
programme.desc: Featuring the latest tik tok sensation, a visit from an angel and a glimpse into the future via a gifted cut-price fortune teller.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Secrets of Size: Atoms to Supergalaxies
programme.desc: Jim encounters ever larger cosmic structures to reveal the latest breakthroughs in understanding of the universe. For example, theres the heliosphere, a vast cloud of solar plasma that surrounds and protects the solar system. Its unique physics help people understand why life is possible. Larger still, Jim comes face to face with our galactic home the Milky Way, a monstrous structure sculpted by the gravitational forces of dark matter.

programme.title: This Is BBC Four
programme.desc: BBC FOUR is the BBC channel for people who want more. More depth, more range, more to stimulate the mind.

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: This edition focuses on places of worship, taking in Preston, County Durham, St Pauls Cathedral and York Minster.

programme.title: Wainwrights Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia sets out on the final stage. Robin Hoods Bay is the goal, but first she must complete the North York Moors and walk through Eskdale, before getting a first glimpse of the North Sea. The woods of Littlebeck and the clifftop finale provide an opportunity to reflect on a great adventure.

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Using 70 years of BBC history archive film, Professor Alice Roberts uncovers how the iconic ancient monument of Stonehenge has been interpreted, argued over and debated by some of Britains leading historians and archaeologists. She reveals how new discoveries would discredit old theories, how astronomers and geologists became involved in the story and why, even after centuries of study, theres still no definitive answer to the mystery of Stonehenge.

programme.title: Julian Barnes: Beyond the Page
programme.desc: Julian Barnes: How a very unconfident man went on to be feted as one of the best of British novelists of the 1980s. Scaling the heights of literary greatness with works such as Flauberts Parrot and The Sense of an Ending, hes now penned his final novel. The award-winning writer talks to the BBCs culture editor, Katie Razzall about his career, his thoughts on death and saying farewell to his novels and readers.

programme.title: The Sense of an Ending
programme.desc: A business owner reunites with his first love after a letter and a diary force him to confront the past.

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks to...
programme.desc: Julian Barnes won the 2011 Man Booker Prize for `The Sense of an Ending and his latest novel, `Levels of Life is a memoir of bereavement following the loss of his wife to cancer. Here he talks to Mark Lawson about love, death, memory and grief.

programme.title: My Garden of a Thousand Bees
programme.desc: Wildlife film-maker Martin Dohrn is bee obsessed. He has found over 60 species in his Bristol garden, from giant bumblebees to scissor bees the size of a mosquito, which he films with mind-blowing results. Dohrn can identify individual insects and forms a remarkable relationship with one leaf-cutter bee he follows through its entire life.

programme.desc: How the senseless murder of a pensioner was finally solved, thanks to DNA preserved in her clutched hand. And a sisters fight for justice in the UKs oldest case of double jeopardy.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A massive multimillion-pound unit in London is up for grabs, but what to do with it? In Norfolk, Jacquis view of the beautiful countryside is being blocked. And in Cheshire, theres hardly room for Dion to move.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross is in Hele, near Exeter, where experts Caroline Hawley and John Cameron guide the reds and blues as they go in search of the best buys before battling it out at auction in Bristol. Meanwhile, Charlie visits the home of Bristol Blue Glass and learns how glass making hasnt changed much since the Roman era.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.desc: Will Kirk and Christina Trevanion take their mobile auction house on the road.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: From Bletchley Parks wartime secrets to a beloved brooch and the art of wig making - join Dom and Will on another restoration road trip.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. Shes a total beginner, but the science is firmly on her side. Getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a retired couple find their dream Devon home by the sea and meets a Dartmoor family who map family-friendly walks in the National Park for all to enjoy.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the days game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: The three strongest players from both heats come face to face for the first time. The six semi-finalists collide, ready to be pushed to their limits and fight for a space in the final.

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: Clive heads north from Lazios capital, Rome, to the dramatic valleys of Italys badlands, out of which rise the fairy-tale citadel of Civita di Bagnoregio - Lazios ghost town. Meeting one of the 20 remaining residents, Clive finds out what its like to be outnumbered by a million annual visitors, and he explores the ancient tunnels beneath the town to discover why its slowly crumbling to dust.

programme.title: Big Little Journeys
programme.desc: A tiny female turtle hatchling struggles out of her egg and emerges onto the roadside. She heads out alone, searching for a wetland pond she can call home. However, she faces an almost impossible task: crossing Highway 60.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Featuring a valuable collection of jewellery found in a rubbish tip, a Stanley Spencer sketch of the owners father who was the baker in Cookham, a brooch presented by the Prince of Wales to his tiger-hunting host and a silver beaker which was filled with gold coins as a bonus to a whaling captain. All are among the items brought to the experts at Cliveden in Buckinghamshire. And Michael Aspel is surprised to discover that a champions boxing belt was awarded to the owners mother.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In the magnificent Truro Cathedral, Paul Martin and experts David Barby and David Fletcher lead a team of valuers hoping to find the very best items to take to auction. It is a busy day and hundreds of hopeful people have turned up in the hope that they will be one of the chosen ones. Paul takes a break from the valuations to discover the beautiful Minack Theatre, built almost single-handedly by one woman on the Cornish coast.

programme.desc: Former winners Amy Gledhill, Jacqui Joseph, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Tim Vine test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Champions.

programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.desc: When a gang of kids steals the Krusty Krabs grill, SpongeBob and Mr Krabs go to extreme lengths to win it back.

programme.desc: Steve Backshall reveals the supersucker of all sharks, the nurse shark. This shark has a taste for shellfish and armoured crustaceans. In Mexico, Steve finds one resting amongst the coral.

Then he heads to South island, to get an aerial view of one of the biggest animals on Earth; before driving inland to meet a very curious bird armed with deadly intelligence. Finally, Steve returns to the ocean in search of sperm whales and uses his kayak to get an awe-inspiring close-up view.

How can she sneak away from the cat? This episode has fun with the letters A, C, P, T and N and the words cat, can, pat, tap, cap, nap and dog.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster gets overwhelmed on Happy to Help Day when he promises to help Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, the In Chicks and the Council of Cuteness but cant finish any of the jobs.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Its a hot day in Playtime Towers, so Bella helps Lift cool down with a paper fan. Together, they visit Hettie in the Ice Disco, who has a flapping paper fan dance!

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its Broccoli and his floret Broc performing their phenomenal ventriloquist act.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Its Bings third birthday! Bing shows Sula, Pando and Coco how to do the waka-oke. When Pando doesnt do it properly, a frustrated Bing hides under his blankey. Flop gently helps Bing to understand that Pando likes to do things differently to him.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `toothbrush, and goes on a whirlwind word adventure. See a red toothbrush, a blue toothbrush, a new toothbrush, a fuzzy toothbrush and even a giant toothbrush.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu gets upset when she tries to show off her new shoes and no one appreciates them. Barnaby helps her understand that the opinions of others dont matter as long as she likes her shoes.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a man whose car is stuck in the mud. The man tries pulling the car free with a rope but it wont budge. When the man goes off to phone for help, the Twirlywoos step in and try pulling the car out of the mud themselves.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit an enchanted castle to make a magic wand and fly on a broomstick. Meanwhile, The Great Tumbleeny performs some magic himself, with varying success.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po take turns to ride Pos scooter and then they take turns to play with the Tiddlytubbies. Children in Tummy Tales take turns to press apples into juice.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Minnie, Luna, Oliver, River, Theo and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside Nigels bag are a toy pig, a toy sheep and a toy chicken and other toys that are themed around things you might find at a farm.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a shopping centre with her friend George and plays a game of I Spy.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: James, Noah and Aaron are on a mission to tidy up the city centre garden for a party. They work with Chatterpots friends - a rake, a spade and Wheely the wheelbarrow.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca has lost her healthy, fruity snacks. Murphi cant find them either, but he does find a huge portrait of his great grandma with the big eyes. This gives him an eye-dea for a make - a pair of silly eyeglasses! The Mini Monsters make gorgeous-looking muffins - but not from the kind of sponge youd expect. Rebecca gets a call on the Magic Monster Mirror from her aunt, who has lost her ducks - she decides to replace them with a quiet, peaceful model version instead.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas embark on an underground chase in pursuit of a burrowing Artichokes.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo test the limits of Dads patience, when they become restless while waiting for their takeaway order.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is reading the Squirrels one of his favourite comics. They are eager to know the ending, but the comic was discontinued. Luckily, Duggee has the comic badge, so he can show the Squirrels how to create their own ending.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Old MacDonald
programme.desc: Lord Fingal sings about all the animals as he takes the Squirrels on a tour around Old MacDonalds farm.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the city and along the river to a place where they can draw pictures. On the way they discover birds called cormorants, go as fast a rollercoaster, find a big compass on the ground, and play a game.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Kyra and Khloe have some garden fun planned. First, they visit a garden centre to buy some new plants, before heading back home to plant them. Its hard work, but Kyra is sure her flowers will grow to be nice and big.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Eddies mum is Italian and has taught him how to make a traditional Italian pizza. Eddie makes a delicious cheese and olive pizza using homemade dough and a tasty tomato sauce. His friends are in for a real treat when they see that Eddies mum cooks the pizza in their traditional pizza oven. He tops his pizza with black olives, but some of Eddies friends arent too sure about olives when they taste them on their own.

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: James finds out all about some very cool sea creatures - starfish. Chantelle is with Dawood to investigate different types of starfish - one with chocolate chips and another that looks like a cushion.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Tinks is a disability assistance dog for her owner Charli, who has a condition that makes it painful for her to pick things up or to push and pull things. Tinks uses her superfetch skills to help Charli every day. The smallest of the Dog Squad gang, Tinks is a little Lhasa Apso with lots of big dog energy. Charli has been invited to a party but is feeling a bit nervous about going.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Mangshan Pit Viper and White-Blotched River Stingray
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a pit viper and a white-blotched river stingray.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle prepares to sing his special line, but time runs out. Evie knows how to help.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona learns an amazing new skill.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. Ruby sings a diva style ballad about the thing she loves more than anything else, school.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes attempts to write a song about his love for the Sunhouse are interrupted by the rest of the gang. Yukee must find a way to help him finish his song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels stumble upon a room full of old radio equipment and discover that Duggee used to host his own show! Duggee reminisces about his radio days running Clubhouse Radio, playing music, telling jokes, chatting to callers and reading the news. It was the most popular show on air, until a new rival show appeared and replaced him: the tellingly-named Cheese Radio.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `bowl and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different bowls and explore how they are used. There are red bowls, green bowls and filled up with baked bean bowls.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Its Numberblock Seventeens area art challenge! Who can find the area of a canvas?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: When A gets hiccups, she cant say her own name - but Magic E and friends sing a song to help her remember.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a theme park and ride on a rollercoaster. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble and the Tumble Toys are very tired and need to sleep - except for the puppy!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po makes lots of silly, funny sounds with the levers and buttons in Home Dome and then the Tubby Phone rings for a silly tubby phone dance.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Bison has a problem: he lost his brother! Will the Patchwork Pals find a way to help him?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Raa Raa and Ooo Ooo are trying to find out who is the funniest animal in the Jingly Jangly Jungle. However, they soon realise that they are much funnier when they are together.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Chug Patrol is always in control, but when a thunder storm comes to Chuggington, Wilson cant control his fear. Wilson must face his fears and weather the storm to help a friend in need.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Its autumn in Will Barrows garden and a cheeky squirrel is finding lots of places to bury his nuts. Newsreader Arthur Sleep has trouble with his important newsreaders chair.

programme.title: Andys Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy goes in search of the woolly rhino, travelling back 50,000 years to a snowy prehistoric mountain range. But can he find the rhinos favourite food and get back to his time-travelling clock before it returns to the museum?

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers are five friends who travel through space exploring fantastic planets with one big mission: to discover, play and collect all the games in the universe.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts head to the kelp forest to visit Shellingtons sister, Pearl, and her newborn son Periwinkle - only to find the forest endangered by an infestation of kelp-eating urchins!

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Surprising Sisters
programme.desc: When Flopsy and Mopsy insist on accompanying Peter on one of his adventures, he discovers that there may be more to his twin sisters than he thought.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is having fun looking at nature in the garden. When Roly wants to join in, she tells him he needs to be really quiet, but he gets overexcited, shouting so loudly that he scares a lovely butterfly away. Norrie is put out because Roly did the opposite of what she said. When Roly asks what an opposite is, Norrie struggles to explain. Luckily, Duggee has his Opposites Badge and helps the squirrels understand by using lots of fun examples.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Zigzag needs to wear an eye patch, Vida enlists the help of Molasses, the seeing eye snail, but Zigzag refuses.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato has to keep Raisins most precious button safe from Evil Pea.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy cheats at hide-and-seek by putting a tracking device on the Ducklings.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo need to get Felix, Dot and Nans giant pie across the lake at once. But Felix wants the pie, and Dot wants Felix. It will take some creative puzzle-solving!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want Dad all to themselves and will not let Mum have a smoochy kiss. They try to hide him all around the house, but Mum is desperate to spend a little romance time with her husband.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.sub-title: Five Piris and a Little Egg
programme.desc: The Piris agree to mind a package for Shirley but discover its a precious egg! Watching over Eggy turns out to be exhausting, but then Shirley returns with unexpected news.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While Dad and his mates pull out some tree stumps in the garden, Bluey, Bingo and Muffin turn one stump into a nail salon. When it comes time to pull out the girls stump, Dad must strike a deal.

programme.title: Andys Dino Island
programme.sub-title: Andy, the T-Rex and the Volcano
programme.desc: When the ground begins to shake on Dino Island, Jen and Andy suspect the volcano is to blame. They set out on an adventure to discover the cause. Along the way, Andy meets a T-rex.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle and Doug give Evie a birthday gift, and the Brooklyn-Bells discover Mrs Hobbs new rollerskating hobby. Evie makes a wish at her party to receive a card from her birth dad.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi promises her dads she will go straight to bed while Aunty T babysits. But when Aunty T suggests night-time ice cream, Rafi makes a wish and her bed magically flies them out into Manchester! At first, its exciting, but soon Rafi becomes sleepy and wants to go home. Suddenly, the wish runs out and the bed drops onto a tree. Luckily, Aunty T uses her umbrella to magic them safely back. Rafi realises bedtime is bedtime for a reason.

programme.sub-title: Its Time for a Spring Clear Out
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran are having a spring clear out, but when Gran Gran wants to clear out her old jumper, JoJo is distraught, she loves that snuggly jumper. Determined to keep it, JoJo hides the old jumper.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Sarah is keen to adopt a sea creature, but she doesnt get her first choice.

programme.desc: The Froglets produce a purple blob out of the Top Hat, and Tiny loves it! The blob is noisy and bouncy, and when Tiny and Small discover it can split in two, and the two can split into four, and four into eight and so on, soon all the caves are alive with bouncing blobs!

programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa play dress up.

programme.desc: Emma prepares the perfect Christmas for her family, but her unrelenting obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. In the 1970s, as Emma recovers from a heart attack, an oblivious Paula doubles down on her love affair with Jim.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A pedestrian is rushed to AandE with multiple life-threatening injuries after being injured in a hit-and-run. An acrobat has a damaged wrist. And a 93-year-old has a broken leg.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Sixty-five-year-old Heather is rushed to Queens after shes found collapsed in the park. Deanna, long serving member of the team, speaks of her previous struggles with addiction.

programme.desc: Keswick Mountain Rescue team are called to help a fallen scrambler with head and neck injuries whos been precariously lodged at the edge of a waterfall for hours. And a marathon brings its own challenges as temperatures soar towards 30 degrees.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane is stranded at the bar by her fiance, who has reunited with his ex-wife, Sam feels sorry for her, and offers her a job at the bar.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane is teasing about the air-headed women Sam dates and sends him looking for a date from a higher cerebral plane. Also, an old customer insists he must see the former owner to discuss his personal problems.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carlas fiery temper almost costs Sam the bar when she attacks an obnoxious Yankees fan. Diane offers to help by suggesting Carla seek anger management.

programme.desc: Debras mum convinces Ray to throw her daughter a surprise party.

programme.desc: The kids ruin Rays dictionary.

programme.desc: When Debra accepts a position at Franks lodge to help plan a member recruitment party, Ray begins to resent how much time she is spending away from home.

programme.title: Frasier

Plucking up the courage to speak with her, he succeeds in getting a date. Meanwhile, Martin prepares for his fishing weekend with his sons at the log cabin they stayed at when they were younger. When they arrive at the cabin, they meet the woman who has sent Frasiers pulse racing.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier turns Daphnes simple wedding into a lavish affair, and Niles doesnt realise that his new girlfriend is a prostitute and enjoys documenting the time they spend together.

programme.title: Padmaavat
programme.desc: A queen, happily married to a Rajput ruler, has a tyrant Sultan enter their life. The Sultan becomes obsessed with the queen and then calls war on her kingdom.

programme.desc: Rajkumar, an aimless youth, works for a drug baron and is sent to kill a rival dealer. His life is changed forever when he meets Chanda, and has no idea she is the adopted daughter of the man he is supposed to kill.

programme.desc: The police use a criminal to assassinate an underworld don responsible for the deaths of many children, only he falls in love.

programme.desc: With a bond like chalk and cheese, Swati and Siddharth are forced into a marriage union; as they enter a new chapter of life, will love find its way?

programme.desc: The kids ruin Rays dictionary.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Birthing Video
programme.desc: Monica prepares for a sexy Valentines Day, but Chandler is too shell-shocked after accidentally viewing Rachels birthing video. Meanwhile, Phoebe gives Joey the worlds most cheerful dog.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Tells Rachel
programme.desc: A guilt-ridden Joey finally confesses his love for Rachel to the flabbergasted Ross, while Chandler becomes nervous when Phoebes handsome new boyfriend Don shares numerous personality traits with Monica.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tea Leaves
programme.desc: In an effort to restore normality to their friendship, Rachel tells Joey a big lie about her unborn child. Meanwhile, Phoebes tea leaves reveal that she will soon encounter the man of her dreams.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe brings her annoyingly upbeat boyfriend to Mr and Mrs Gellers 35th wedding anniversary party. Meanwhile, Ross is astounded when his parents ask him and the pregnant Rachel to pretend to be married, in order to avoid a potential scandal.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is thrilled when his name is used in a `Soap Opera Digest crossword puzzle. He is reluctant to let the magazine do a profile of him, but Rachel tries to persuade him to reconsider so that he can mention her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby Shower
programme.desc: Rachel panics when she realises how unprepared she is to care for an infant. She reluctantly agrees to allow her frustrating mother Sandra to move in to help her, upsetting Ross. Meanwhile, Monica tries to atone for forgetting to invite Sandra to the baby shower, and Chandler and Ross exhaust themselves by repeatedly playing a confusing game called Bamboozle to help Joey prepare to audition as a game show host.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cooking Class
programme.desc: When Monicas cooking at the restaurant receives a harsh write-up in a newspaper, she goes to a criticism class taught by the reviewer, hoping to convince him to reconsider his opinion. Meanwhile, Rachel gets upset when a cute salesgirl flirts with Ross while he and Rachel are shopping for baby supplies, and nervous about a major job interview, Chandler receives some questionable coaching from Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Is Late
programme.desc: When Rachels pregnancy goes beyond the expected delivery date, the pain makes her increasingly irritable and willing to try anything that might speed up the babys arrival. Meanwhile, Monica and Phoebe bet on the date of the birth, while Joey can only take one guest to the premiere of his film, and chooses Chandler. Unfortunately, Chandler falls asleep during the screening.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Has a Baby
programme.desc: Rachel waits in the hospital maternity ward with an anxious Ross, but to her dismay she is put in a semi-private room and then, the heavily pregnant Janice, is allocated to share the room with her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Has a Baby
programme.desc: Rachel waits in the hospital maternity ward with an anxious Ross. Numerous other pregnant women are wheeled off to give birth while Rachels painful labour continues. Elsewhere in the hospital, Phoebe is attracted to a handsome patient, Cliff, and asks Joey to pose as Dr Drake Ramoray to interrogate Cliff about his background.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No One Proposes
programme.desc: Due to a misunderstanding, Rachel finds herself engaged to Joey instead of Ross shortly after giving birth to her and Rosss baby girl. Meanwhile, Joey cant help taking a look as Rachel tries unsuccessfully to nurse her baby. Inspired to have a baby of their own, Monica and Chandler retire to a hospital cupboard for a little privacy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joeys futile attempts to gain Rosss forgiveness for mistakenly asking Rachel to marry him only result in emergency hospitalisation. Meanwhile, an exhausted Rachel desperately searches for a solution to end baby Emmas non-stop crying.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachels constant calls to Emmas paediatrician force him to reject her as a patient. Meanwhile, Chandler plans to move to Tulsa for work, but worries about whether or not career-minded Monica is still willing to join him, and Joey and Phoebe agree to find each other companions for a double date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe fears losing her latest prospective boyfriend Mike and her self-doubt is not helped by Ross careless comments. Meanwhile, Chandler is in Tulsa on a work assignment, and when Monica surprises him in his hotel room, she mistakenly thinks that he has a sexual fetish for sharks.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Phoebes Birthday Dinner
programme.desc: While Phoebe and Joey wait at a fancy restaurant for their friends arrival to celebrate Phoebes birthday, Rachel is delayed because she is afraid to leave Emma with a babysitter. Monica and Chandler also fail to arrive on time after having a row about Chandlers urge to resume smoking and then giving in to other urges.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Male Nanny
programme.desc: Ross mocks Rachels new male nanny, and Chandler becomes upset when Monica says that somebody from her job is the funniest guy shes ever met.

programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Goes Back to Work

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe and Mike parent baby rats born in her cabinet, Rachel gets steamed after Monica invites a rival to her birthday bash, and Joey likes Emmas new nanny.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: While Rachel tries to sort out her mixed feelings for her handsome colleague Gavin, Ross recruits Chandler to help him meet attractive women and make Rachel jealous. programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: In South Yorkshire, Phil and his wife Ali host an evening fit for the A-list, theres romance in the air for Erica and Nigel, and art lovers Stephen and Kevin create a sophisticated trifle.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Joe and Lana are looking to live in the suburbs with their three young kids. To realise this dream, Drew and Jonathan must first conduct a major makeover on their current home.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Leon wants to get wed on a beach in Weymouth. But whilst Leon slowly gets his wacky wedding in motion, his traditional bride Kirsty dreams of a romantic fairy-tale day at a venue that means something special to her.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Sam wants a witchy wedding in the woods, but traditional bride Ezra has her heart set on a fairytale event. Sams ghoulish gathering might turn out to be a real Halloween nightmare.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: After a 25-year break from working as a chauffeur, EmpireCLS CEO David Seelinger goes under cover to see if his business is running smoothly.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The Rocket Fizz Soda Pop and Candy Shops President Robert Powells makes a discovery about his inventory system while under cover.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits the Seascape restaurant in the seaside village of Islip in Long Island, New York. Owner Peter Matthews has been struggling for seven years to make the business viable and now hes nearly reached the end of the line.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon tries to help put a deserted California restaurant back on the map; the restaurants owner does not want to accept the changes, which leads to a screaming match.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg Davies and Alex Horne set their captive band of comics on a variety of humiliating missions involving cling film, fashion for insects, and face-based shape-shifting.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host a festive edition. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican take on Sean Lock and David Mitchell. With guest David ODoherty.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan and Joe Lycett take on Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas. Jimmy Carr hosts. Rachel Riley looks after the numbers. Christopher Bliss joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark and Sophie are finally beginning to go steady when Sophie is moved away from London for work. Meanwhile, Merry, an eccentric old friend from university, offers Jeremy the chance to manage a pub she owns. When she has a nervous breakdown however, Jeremy is forced to consider getting her sectioned, and soon realises that sectioning could be a good way of ridding his life of other problem people.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeremy plans to throw a secret magic mushroom party in the flat, where he hopes to seduce his ex-girlfriend, Big Suze. However, when Mark suddenly comes home from work with gastric flu, Jeremys plans go to pieces. Meanwhile, in a moment of panic, Super Hans does some violent damage to the toilet door, but the builder they call in is equally unhinged.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: Jay banishes Ants backside buddy, while Glen checks out Erikas checklist. Alice blows away Cheryls windy devil and Sketch sorts out Jasons sambuca stunt scar.

programme.desc: Greg Davies and Alex Horne set their captive band of comics on a variety of humiliating missions involving cling film, fashion for insects, and face-based shape-shifting.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Joe DiDomizio is the president and CEO of Hudson Group, a travel retailer that operates newsstands, bookshops, cafes and speciality stores in 70 airports and other transportation terminals throughout North America.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Utah Jazz CEO Greg Millers team spirit is tested when he is asked to be a human basketball hoop while he is undercover.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Somerset, property developer Norman and Dees Indian cuisine proves a little too spicy. Ashley lets Sophie down by wearing a creased shirt, and Brett and Vicky serve up an American menu.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cornwall, artistic Zaya and Damian serve a modern British menu. Property developers Kelly and Jon show off their refurbished mansion. And Emma and Gavin host a fun-filled Cornish night.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Drew and Jonathan are here to help Nick and Julie leave their small, cluttered home for a larger space. Though first, the brothers need to revamp the cramped space in order for it to be ready for the market.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Fantasy fan Evan plans to wage war on his wedding, but his emotional bride-to-be Zoe wants a low-key affair. The question remains as to whether this noble groom will make his bride feel like a queen.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Hapless Liam wants his greatest passion to take flight with a darts-inspired wedding. Will his arrows extravaganza hit the target, or prove a load of old bull?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Rick Forman, founder and CEO of New Jersey-based Forman Mills, a discount clothing superstore, goes incognito among his own staff posing as a contestant on a television reality show.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Stella and Dot CEO Jessica Herrin goes under cover to find the real gems working in her company, and learns one employees true feelings about corporate management.

programme.desc: Lelas is a fine dining restaurant in Pomona, California, run by inexperienced owner Lela and young chef Ricky. The fatal combination of her ineptitude and his arrogance has resulted in a business so rocky that its about to close down for good.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Capt. Glenn lays down the law with the crew after not following his rules; the tension between Gary and Chase boils over during a heated confrontation.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Jamali Maddix is tasked with decorating the Taskmaster caravan, while Sarah Kendall bites into a deep-fried kiwi fruit and Mike Wozniak leaves Greg Davies in a state of horrified delirium.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts while Sean Lock and Bob Mortimer take on Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont. With Adam Buxton, Joe Wilkinson, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Marks plans for an enjoyable and productive week off work go out the window when his sister comes to stay. However, its then Jeremys turn to be concerned when Mark realises he has unexpected feelings for Jeremys ex-girlfriend, Big Suze.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Sophie and her new gay friend, Dom, drag Mark off to a gay club, where he pretends to take drugs with the others. Meanwhile, Jeremy is on jury service, and starts an ill-advised affair with the female defendant.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: Its Halloween in the pop-up parlour. Sketch slays Fionas zombie and helps Spike with a monster of a tattoo, while Glen gives magician George a frightening new friend.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Jamali Maddix is tasked with decorating the Taskmaster caravan, while Sarah Kendall bites into a deep-fried kiwi fruit and Mike Wozniak leaves Greg Davies in a state of horrified delirium.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Its Phils birthday and he desperately wants the years must-have gadget. Meanwhile, Mitchell tries to find his inner mean machine.

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray buys WrestleMania tickets to spend time with Barry and Adam, only to find they are fakes and they cant get in.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley is determined to follow the advice in her parenting books, but Phil and Claire think their old methods are better. Manny is set to direct Jays dog bed commercial in hopes of winning his ex-girlfriend back.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire needs to get everyone out of the house before they make her look bad in front of a reporter from an important womens business magazine. Jay invites Manny to a wine tasting in an attempt to spend some quality time together.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Ventura Harbor in Southern California to help a family-run Greek restaurant facing big problems that hasnt taken a profit for two years. Makis Mikelatos and his wife Lynn have run the restaurant, the Greek at the Harbor, for 17 years, but Makis - who works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, and rarely takes a holiday - is exhausted, and if he doesnt slow down hes in danger of collapse.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: A publishing company is interested in Beverlys cookbook, but they dont like how she writes. Barry and Erica cannot stop fighting as roommates and they make themselves crazy, and Geoff comes up with a housing plan that changes everything.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Hoping to outdo the Kremp family, Beverly decides the Goldbergs must do an even better family holiday card; a prank war with Barry and JTP quickly spirals out of control.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The arrival of Mannys father stirs tension between Gloria and Jay, Claire has to rely on a sexy fireman in order to help Phil get to the hospital and Lily is being sleep-trained by Mitchell and Cameron.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell sows the seed that one of Jays buddies is secretly gay, and Claire wages war against a remote control.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA

Alone at the helm, and still mourning the loss of his father, Mike has taken his eye off the ball and the restaurant has gone to pot.

programme.desc: Intimacy Week reaches boiling point as one relationship threatens to implode, while a bride makes a decision that leaves her groom heartbroken.

programme.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai
programme.desc: British POWs are ordered by their Japanese captors to construct a bridge of strategic importance and are happy to sabotage and delay the progress until their commanding officers orders them to continue the work unhindered to its completion, but are his actions tantamount to collaborating with the enemy?

programme.title: Enemy Mine
programme.desc: As a devastating conflict rocks the galaxy, space pilot Willis Davidge finds himself stranded on a barren planet with Jeriba Shigan, a member of the alien race Earth is at war with. Initially hostile, the two come to depend on each other as they attempt to survive.

programme.title: See How They Run
programme.desc: In 1950s London, a world-weary inspector and an eager rookie constable take on a puzzling whodunit within the glamorously sordid world of underground theatre, investigating a mysterious murder at their own peril.

programme.title: A Few Good Men
programme.desc: A young and impetuous naval lieutenant and a more experienced officer are given the job of defending two marines accused of murder. As the two officers come to terms with one another, their investigation uncovers some sinister secrets and collusion at the highest level. The marines commanding officer certainly has no intention of making the truth public.

programme.title: Boiling Point
programme.desc: On the busiest night of the year at one of the hottest restaurants in London, charismatic and commanding head chef Andy Jones balances along a knifes edge as multiple personal and professional crises threaten to destroy everything hes worked for.

programme.title: The Card Counter
programme.desc: William Tell is a gambler and former serviceman who sets out to reform a young man seeking revenge on a mutual enemy from their past. Tell just wants to play cards. His spartan existence on the casino trail is shattered when he is approached by Cirk, a vulnerable and angry young man seeking help to execute his plan for revenge on a military colonel. Tell sees a chance at redemption through his relationship with Cirk.

programme.desc: In 1950s London, a world-weary inspector and an eager rookie constable take on a puzzling whodunit within the glamorously sordid world of underground theatre, investigating a mysterious murder at their own peril.

programme.title: A Few Good Men
programme.desc: A young and impetuous naval lieutenant and a more experienced officer are given the job of defending two marines accused of murder. As the two officers come to terms with one another, their investigation uncovers some sinister secrets and collusion at the highest level. The marines commanding officer certainly has no intention of making the truth public.

programme.title: Boiling Point
programme.desc: On the busiest night of the year at one of the hottest restaurants in London, charismatic and commanding head chef Andy Jones balances along a knifes edge as multiple personal and professional crises threaten to destroy everything hes worked for.

programme.title: The Card Counter
programme.desc: William Tell is a gambler and former serviceman who sets out to reform a young man seeking revenge on a mutual enemy from their past. Tell just wants to play cards. His spartan existence on the casino trail is shattered when he is approached by Cirk, a vulnerable and angry young man seeking help to execute his plan for revenge on a military colonel. Tell sees a chance at redemption through his relationship with Cirk.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes care packages for her two daughters in college; she sends them homemade breakfast tarts, nut and seed brittle, salt and pepper crackers and cherry gummies; Rees sons get involved, too, shopping for fun treats to send to their sisters.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina makes dinner to celebrate the arrival of her friends newborn son. Theres a delicious nursery food menu and an incredible setting to match. Theres Truffle Popcorn with drinks followed by Spicy Turkey Meatballs, Strawberry Rhubarb Crisp and Smores around the fire pit. The whole meal is served outside under incredible garden lights designed by Inas friend Greg Yale.

programme.desc: The contestants bake sweet custard tarts, mini pear pies and finally a towering collection of pies from any pastry or pastries they like.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson, and they set out to sample the delights of Northumberland. They learn how mead is made on Holy Island, before James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, and they both enjoy a Puffin cruise at Cockett Island. Theres a trip to a lobster hatchery too, before James cooks local speciality Singing Hinnies on another breathtaking beach.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen for a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm-up week. James serves up proper biker food, pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits local organic juice producers. James picks up some local Manx kippers and makes a delicious soup, before meeting TT legend John McGuinness. Finally, he treats John and his team to some tasty lamb chops with hispi cabbage and an olive glaze.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In San Diego, an all-day breakfast spot serving creme brulee French toast, in Portland, corned beef hash and chipped beef, in San Francisco, lamb merquez dog.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Portland, Ore., a chef who butchers, cures, preserves and smokes everything in-house, in San Carlos, Calif., a neighborhood spot featuring burgers, cheese steaks and signature sandwiches.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North

Its an area that Si knows well, having holidayed there many times throughout his childhood, and he is keen to introduce Dave to some of the producers and chefs he has got to know over the years. Starting in the seaside town of Amble, where they eat fresh seafood from the North Sea, they head to a potato farm at the foot of the Cheviot Hills that grows nine different varieties of potato.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree spills the beans on 10 things she loves about her husband, Ladd, and the food he loves most; Spatchcock barbecue chicken with apple brown sugar barbecue sauce; waffle fries with sour cream and onion dip; spoon cake with gooey chocolate frosting.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree cooks Tex-Mex marvels and fills the freezer with family favourites; she makes a big pot of green chili pork stew and a batch of Fiesta Mac and Cheese; her All-Purpose Tex-Mex Veggies go in soft tacos, veggie taco salad and quesadillas.

programme.desc: Casey heads to Charleston for epic low country chow. He takes on the Belt Buster Challenge, in which he must consume a 1 kg burger, 1.4 kg of nachos, tater tots, and a 340 ml milkshake in 20 minutes.

programme.desc: Its the fish course and with a seafood specialist in the kitchen the competition is stepping up a gear. Tom is hoping hell be able to elevate the humble mackerel to the standards required for the Wimbledon banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon related recipe, who will come out on top? Andy Clatworthy is using his passion for historical cooking to reinvent a dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are honouring Wimbledon and the British summer with their technically challenging strawberry desserts. Only the two highest scorers will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs compete for a place in the national finals. To get there theyll have to impress the formidable panel of judges: Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom Parker is eager to impress with a salad that includes 10 elements. Meanwhile, Paul Askew, hopes his traditional techniques will triumph over his younger competitors, and Ellis Barrie is taking a risk with a creative cucumber and oyster starter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen gets even tenser as the three newcomers begin to understand the perfection and skill the veteran chef demands. Ellis Barrie attempts to create the flavour of a summer barbecue.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and the pressure is intense in the kitchen. In the hope of impressing the veteran judge, Paul Askew is serving an extremely personal main course based on his mothers favourite food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul cant afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. Its Phils third time in the competition and this year hes determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, hes serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledons Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, its the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. Shes takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpsons also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughmans lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Laceys pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britains most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined this week by tennis greatest entertainer, Mansour Bahrami.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With Finals Week ahead of them, judge Andi Oliver first summons the chefs to Wimbledon to take a look at where they could be cooking if they win. The judges are joined by guest judge Sue Barker, host of the Wimbledon coverage for more than 20 years.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt for archaeology from the more recent past as they follow the search for artefacts from World War II. They join marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise the once-in-a-lifetime find of a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. More than 2,500 Barracudas were in service during the war but not a single complete plane survives today. Naoíse Mac Sweeney joins the post excavation to reveal how the find could help bring this rare aircraft back to life.

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: A walk through the iconic mausoleum complex, located in India and commissioned in 1632 by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan to house the remains of his beloved wife.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: River Hunters sees Rick and river-searching expert Beau searching the unexplored rivers and waterways of Britain, unearthing treasures from the past which reveal how our various ancestors used to live.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Norwich, the UKs most Tudor city, Professor Alice Roberts learns about religious intolerance, uncovers the Norwich FC canarys origins and experiences humiliating Tudor justice.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, Britains most Regency town, Professor Alice Roberts attends a period society ball, investigates social hierarchy and explores the Regency fondness for enemas.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.sub-title: Chinese Revolution - Modern World, Modern Emperors
programme.desc: The history of modern China is told from the birth of the republic in 1911, through the civil war, the bloody Japanese occupation, the rise to power of Mao and the Communist Party, the Cultural Revolution, to the present day.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: Explore how the Polynesians spread across the Pacific and examine the cultures they took with them while constructing elaborate temples to their beliefs.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Norfolk, flint tools unearthed recently push the earliest human occupation back by 200,000 years, to around one million years ago. In Orkney an early farm yields glimpses of our ancestors earliest religious beliefs and customs.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in London, as she attempts to make sense of the world-changing events during the short but eventful Restoration period. She learns the violent and vengeful truth behind Charles IIs carefully crafted public image.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Portsmouth, Alice Roberts learns about Nelsons dark side, the heat of the battle at Trafalgar, the role of the Navy in the British Empire, and the lives of sex workers.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits Sherwood Forest and the Peak District as he sets out to discover the real King John.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorsets Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the dashing young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists search Orkney and the North of Britain, where they discover a Roman altar and a 5,000-year-old temple.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Marden Henge: The communal sweat lodges and feasting remains that illuminate the lost rituals of Stonehenge. Durotriges: A glimpse into the bizarre animal sacrifice rituals offered to their gods by a mysterious Celtic tribe of the first century BC.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: Explore how the Polynesians spread across the Pacific and examine the cultures they took with them while constructing elaborate temples to their beliefs.

programme.title: Empire Builders

programme.title: Unearthed: Ancient Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Homicide detective Rod Demery and forensic scientist Turi King investigate a century-old dismembered body, found in a cave, and deduce who may have killed him.

programme.title: Unearthed: Ancient Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Homicide detective Rod Demery and scientist Professor Turi King study an 800-year-old partial skeleton found in Berwick, Scotland. Rod investigates a possible motive of robbery, and Turis injury analysis reveals the victim may have been an archer.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. On three occasions, Highland armies, assisted by the French and the Spanish, attempted to overthrow the King and put a Stuart back on the throne.

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Stronger, Faster, Younger? Britains Steroid Boom
programme.desc: Antoine Allen investigates the rising use of performance enhancing drugs and asks whether the dangers are being ignored. With the inaugural 2026 Enhanced Games taking place in Las Vegas, where drug-assisted former Olympians will attempt to break world records, Tonight travels to their Abu Dhabi training camp to meet British competitors as they begin their doping regimes and asks whether this global event could change the way we view performance enhancing drugs forever.

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: In the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, residents of a farm and the nearby village deal with all the drama, joys and heartbreaks life throws at them.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The working-class residents of Coronation Street see their fair share of drama as they make their way about their daily lives.

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show Live
